introSection: Rail Chase: The Ride (Japanese: レイルチェイス・ザ・ライド) was a steel enclosed dark ride roller coaster formerly located at Joypolis H. Factory in Naka, Yokohama, Kanagawa, Japan.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride originally opened with Yokohama Joypolis on 20 July 1994. It was promoted by Sega as being the "world's first interactive roller coaster" in marketing.

On 8 July 1995, the ride was updated with a new story and setting entitled "Hero Resurrection" (Japanese: ～英雄復活編～). The ride remained as such until the park's closure in February 2001.

fullText:
Rail Chase: The Ride (Japanese: レイルチェイス・ザ・ライド) was a steel enclosed dark ride roller coaster formerly located at Joypolis H. Factory in Naka, Yokohama, Kanagawa, Japan.

The ride originally opened with Yokohama Joypolis on 20 July 1994. It was promoted by Sega as being the "world's first interactive roller coaster" in marketing.

On 8 July 1995, the ride was updated with a new story and setting entitled "Hero Resurrection" (Japanese: ～英雄復活編～). The ride remained as such until the park's closure in February 2001.
